description: The SARI literature marks the super-Alfvenic boundary of the atlas-stable region.

open_questions.question: How wide is the transition region as M_A approaches 1 from below?

suggested_next_steps.description: Sweep M_A upward on the Atlas equilibrium and measure how the least-stable non-axisymmetric modes behave before continuum overlap.
suggested_next_steps.title: Approach M_A = 1 from below

suggested_next_steps.description: Derive the scaling law for residual growth as M_A approaches one and identify whether H3 delays or sharpens the SARI onset.
suggested_next_steps.title: Transition-width asymptotics

suggested_next_steps.description: Map achievable radial rotation and Alfven speed profiles to determine whether devices can stay safely below the SARI boundary.
suggested_next_steps.title: Rotation-boundary measurement

open_questions.question: Which remaining residuals are real contradictions rather than seeded-scope artifacts?

suggested_next_steps.description: Inspect each remaining high-residual edge against the source PDFs and decide whether the edge, regime tag, or claim scope is wrong.
suggested_next_steps.title: Residual edge review

suggested_next_steps.description: If a residual is real, split the broad claim into separate home-regime and cross-regime claims instead of weakening the evidence.
suggested_next_steps.title: Scope split proposal

remaining_tensions.interpretation: BR_01 still predicts poorly at ev_atlas_kink_zero after allowed rewrites.
GK_01 still predicts poorly at ev_atlas_kink_zero after allowed rewrites.

scope.regime: The SARI literature marks the super-Alfvenic boundary of the atlas-stable region.

title: Atlas's regime ends at M_A=1 where SARI continuum overlap takes over
